howItWorks.intro: This ovulation calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Fertile window = 5 days before ovulation through ovulation day
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: When do I ovulate?
faq.answer: Ovulation typically occurs 14 days before your next period. For a 28-day cycle, that is day 14. For a 32-day cycle, around day 18. Ovulation day varies with cycle length.

faq.question: What is the fertile window?
faq.answer: You can conceive during the 5 days before ovulation and the day of ovulation itself (6 days total). Sperm can survive up to 5 days in the reproductive tract.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: It provides estimates based on average cycle patterns. For more accuracy, combine with basal body temperature tracking, cervical mucus observation, or ovulation predictor kits.

faq.question: What if my cycles are irregular?
faq.answer: Irregular cycles make prediction harder. Use ovulation predictor kits (OPKs) which detect the LH surge 24-36 hours before ovulation for more reliable timing.

faq.question: Is the Ovulation Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Ovulation Calculator?
sections.body: The Ovulation Calculator is a free online tool designed for expecting parents who need quick, accurate calculations in the pregnancy and parenting space. By entering your cycle length, days since last period started, you get instant results including estimated ovulation day, fertile window, next period expected. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated ovulation day right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In pregnancy and parenting, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by expecting parents worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to prepare for parenthood with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your cycle length and need to find the right estimated ovulation day. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Cycle Length (days)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your cycle length in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Days Since Last Period Started
howToSteps.description: Type or select your days since last period started in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your estimated ovulation day, fertile window, next period expected. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your cycle length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
